title: Pollinia scoparia
intro: Pollinia scoparia, also known as Mexican chamomile, is a plant species in the family Asclepiadaceae, native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its clusters of small, white flowers and thick, fleshy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits in treating anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Pollinia scoparia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pollinia scoparia include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
